jokes.text: Mmm! "Hors doovers."
jokes.setup: Homer mispronouncing hors d'oeuvres at their first party
jokes.punchline: The butchered pronunciation reveals his lack of sophistication
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - the mispronunciation is both funny and reveals his working-class background trying to be fancy

jokes.text: -Homer! You promised. -That I wouldn't eat? Never. You lie.
jokes.setup: Marge reminds Homer of his promise
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation and accusation that Marge is lying
jokes.explanation: Homer's selfish logic and immediate deflection to Marge being the liar is classic character comedy

jokes.text: What about that bash with champagne and musicians and holy men? -That was our wedding! -Oh.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to recall parties they've thrown
jokes.punchline: He forgot their own wedding was a party
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Homer's obliviousness, with perfect structure and the deadpan 'Oh' as acknowledgment

jokes.text: -I want to hear your witty banter. -You can't have fun in bed. Oh, son, when you're older, you'll know better.
jokes.setup: Bart wanting to stay up for adult conversation
jokes.punchline: Homer's dark commentary on marriage killing fun
jokes.explanation: Dark adult humor disguised as parental wisdom - subversive and quotable

jokes.text: Oh, baby. Mmm! Yeah.
jokes.setup: Homer making romantic sounds
jokes.punchline: The awkwardness of overhearing parents' intimacy
jokes.explanation: Basic cringe humor. Not particularly clever or well-executed.

jokes.text: You look swell. And the house! You've done.... Whatever!
jokes.setup: Guest trying to compliment the house
jokes.punchline: Gives up mid-compliment with dismissive 'whatever'
jokes.explanation: Awkward social interaction with good economy - the trailing off is realistically uncomfortable

jokes.text: Anybody mind if I serve as bartender? I have a Ph.D. in mixology.
jokes.setup: Ned offering to bartend
jokes.punchline: Claims to have a doctorate in mixing drinks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned Flanders - overly formal and educational about everything, even bartending

jokes.text: College boy.
jokes.setup: Ned's pretentious mixology doctorate
jokes.punchline: Homer's dismissive, anti-intellectual response
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer anti-intellectualism in just two words - perfect economy and character consistency

jokes.text: Next time put a little alcohol in it. It has three shots of rum, a jigger of bourbon... Well, I do have a warm sense of well-being, and I'm slurring my words.
jokes.setup: Homer complaining there's no alcohol while Ned lists the copious amounts
jokes.punchline: Homer's delayed realization he's already drunk
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and Homer's obliviousness to his own drunkenness is perfectly timed

jokes.text: Remember when you threw up in the laundry hamper? No.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to remind Homer of his past drunk behavior
jokes.punchline: Homer's flat denial/lack of memory
jokes.explanation: Homer's convenient alcoholic amnesia delivered with perfect economy - one word denial

jokes.text: But I don't remember you being so beautiful.
jokes.setup: Man hitting on Homer's sister-in-law
jokes.punchline: The awkward pickup line
jokes.explanation: Generic awkward pickup line - functions for plot but not particularly clever

jokes.text: A new kind of Mace? Really painful.
jokes.setup: Guest gets maced after inappropriate advance
jokes.punchline: His confused reaction thinking it's a new product
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy reaction with the guest treating mace like a product review

jokes.text: Homer... these ice cubes are made from highly toxic chemicals. A real fly is more sanitary. The look on your face. It's priceless!
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert explaining the fake ice cube's toxicity
jokes.punchline: Homer's delight at the 'priceless' reaction despite the health danger
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy of Homer prioritizing the prank's success over his guest's health - very Simpsons

jokes.text: -Do that cute thing you do. -What? -That thing you know how to do. -What? Go to bed.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to get Bart to perform for guests
jokes.punchline: Bart's repeated confusion leading to Homer just sending him to bed
jokes.explanation: Good escalating awkwardness as Homer's plan to show off Bart completely fails

jokes.text: Look! I'm the funniest guy in the world! Oh, Homer! You're the king.
jokes.setup: Homer doing something embarrassing for attention
jokes.punchline: His desperate self-promotion and the sarcastic/drunk response
jokes.explanation: Homer's desperate need for approval and the mock encouragement he gets from drunk guests

jokes.text: I've been wanting to tell you off for years... I don't know you. Our wives are friends. You just met. You stink! You and your whole lousy operation stinks. I quit!
jokes.setup: Homer getting confrontational with someone
jokes.punchline: The guest pointing out they just met, then Homer quitting his non-job
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity with Homer creating workplace drama at a social gathering with strangers

jokes.text: -Gee, don't quit. -All right.
jokes.setup: Guest's mild response to Homer's dramatic outburst
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately backing down
jokes.explanation: Perfect anticlimax showing Homer's lack of conviction - great economy in the exchange

jokes.text: Not those peanuts. The ones at the bottom. Thanks for inviting me. I had a great time. I apologize for my husband. If you want him to live, roll him onto his stomach.
jokes.setup: Homer being very specific about peanuts while party winds down
jokes.punchline: Medical advice about Homer's unconscious position
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Homer's petty peanut preference and the serious medical situation

jokes.text: Remember, I said 'if.'
jokes.setup: Dr. Hibbert giving medical advice about keeping Homer alive
jokes.punchline: Clarifying he's not committed to Homer surviving
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor with perfect economy - Dr. Hibbert's professional detachment becomes sinister

jokes.text: Why? What did you do?
jokes.setup: Marge saying she's never been so embarrassed
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete obliviousness to his own behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness - he genuinely doesn't understand what he did wrong

jokes.text: I must get out of these wet clothes and into a dry martini.
jokes.setup: Homer's attempt at sophisticated humor
jokes.punchline: Classic old-fashioned joke delivery
jokes.explanation: Old joke but delivered in character. Homer trying to be sophisticated with dated humor.

jokes.text: I must get out of these wet clothes and into a dry martini. -Well said. -Thank you.
jokes.setup: Homer quoting a classic one-liner
jokes.punchline: His pride in the borrowed wit
jokes.explanation: Classic line but Homer's self-congratulation for using it is the real joke

jokes.text: -Well said. -Thank you. -A fly! -I put it there. -You did? -I slipped it in. -Pure hilarity! -Pure Homer! The most whimsical jape of the season.
jokes.setup: Homer recreating the party events in his mind
jokes.punchline: His completely distorted memory of how his pranks were received
jokes.explanation: Brilliant unreliable narrator moment. Homer remembers himself as witty and beloved when he was actually terrible.

jokes.text: But last night, you didn't just cross that line, you threw up on it.
jokes.setup: Marge talking about crossing lines in marriage
jokes.punchline: The literal interpretation with vomit
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor made literal - Marge's frustration expressed through clever wordplay

jokes.text: Oh. You mean inside, don't you?
jokes.setup: Marge saying Homer scarred Bart for life
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation confusion
jokes.explanation: Homer's literal thinking process - he needed clarification about psychological vs physical scarring

jokes.text: I have as much respect for you as I ever did or ever will.
jokes.setup: Homer hoping Bart didn't lose respect for him
jokes.punchline: Bart's statement could mean zero respect
jokes.explanation: Brilliant ambiguous statement from Bart that could be reassuring or devastating - perfect character consistency

jokes.text: "Satan's Boners"... "Good Grief, More Satan's Boners," and... for teens, "It's Not Cool To Fry In Hell."
jokes.setup: Church pamphlet announcements
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous titles, especially 'Satan's Boners'
jokes.explanation: Iconic Simpsons humor - fake pamphlet titles that sound both religious and inappropriate

jokes.text: The Lord and I can't compete with the squeaking of Homer's shoes. Why don't we wait until he sits?
jokes.setup: Homer disrupting church service
jokes.punchline: Reverend's passive-aggressive announcement
jokes.explanation: Good public embarrassment humor with Lovejoy's diplomatic but pointed critique

jokes.text: Marge! What are you doing? Are you insane?!
jokes.setup: Marge signing them up for marriage counseling
jokes.punchline: Homer's panicked overreaction
jokes.explanation: Homer's fear of any self-examination or relationship work - good character consistency

jokes.text: They couldn't call it that if it didn't have a catfish in it.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Catfish Lake retreat
jokes.punchline: Homer's simple logic about naming conventions
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike logic about truth in advertising - sets up his fishing obsession

jokes.text: Come back for more, eh?
jokes.setup: The Simpsons needing a babysitter
jokes.punchline: Sitter's weary recognition of repeat customers
jokes.explanation: Implies the Simpson kids are notorious among babysitters - good worldbuilding humor

jokes.text: Why do you need a sitter? I'm 10 1/2! -I'll give you a taste of me hand.
jokes.setup: Bart arguing he's old enough to babysit
jokes.punchline: Marge's threat of violence
jokes.explanation: Marge's rare display of parental authority with physical threat - breaks her usual patient character

jokes.text: Last resort. Old Grampa, the feeb. The guy who can't be counted on for nothing, nohow. Everyone's against me.
jokes.setup: Being asked to babysit as last option
jokes.punchline: Grampa's self-pitying but accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Grampa - simultaneously pathetic and self-aware about being the family's last choice

jokes.text: You can smoke cigars?
jokes.setup: Reading the list of what kids can/can't do
jokes.punchline: Grampa's surprise at apparently liberal rules
jokes.explanation: Implies Bart and Lisa have created a fake permissive list for gullible Grampa

jokes.text: The biggest catfish around. Weighs over 500 pounds. -Who says that? -They do.
jokes.setup: Claims about General Sherman's size
jokes.punchline: Vague attribution to 'they'
jokes.explanation: Good satire of how fishing legends spread through unnamed authorities

jokes.text: He's freakishly big, though.
jokes.setup: Showing blurry photo of the fish
jokes.punchline: Understatement about something clearly enormous
jokes.explanation: Mild understatement joke. The visual would likely be funnier than the line.

jokes.text: Gentlemen, I am going to catch General Sherman.
jokes.setup: Homer hearing about the legendary fish
jokes.punchline: His immediate grandiose declaration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer overconfidence and his need to announce his intentions dramatically

jokes.text: In these crazy times... who knows what's right or wrong? My gut's telling me: Bleed Gramps dry.
jokes.setup: Lisa questioning if they should take advantage of Grampa
jokes.punchline: Bart's amoral philosophy about exploiting the elderly
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with Bart's pseudo-philosophical justification for elder abuse

jokes.text: My name's Gloria. I'm here because Johnny... hasn't been able to cut it, manwise, for some time. Not that I'd want his odor of sour defeat pressed against me.
jokes.setup: Marriage counseling introductions
jokes.punchline: Gloria's brutally honest and emasculating description
jokes.explanation: Shocking overshare that's both funny and genuinely uncomfortable - great marriage retreat parody

jokes.text: She keeps a filthy house and talks profanely. She's the Queen of the Harpies! -Here's your crown, Your Majesty!
jokes.setup: Johnny's counterattack description of Gloria
jokes.punchline: Gloria's sarcastic acceptance of the title
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the couple's public fight with Gloria's witty comeback

jokes.text: Sometimes Maude underlines passages in my Bible... because she can't find hers. -Lucky you don't keep guns.
jokes.setup: Ned's incredibly minor marriage complaint
jokes.punchline: Homer's dark suggestion about domestic violence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant contrast between Ned's trivial complaint and Homer's extreme reaction - perfect character work

jokes.text: -She's perfect. -What are her faults? -It can be annoying.
jokes.setup: Homer calling Marge perfect when asked about faults
jokes.punchline: His admission that perfection itself is annoying
jokes.explanation: Brilliant paradox - Homer finds Marge's perfection to be her only flaw, which is both sweet and selfish

jokes.text: He blows his nose on towels and puts them back. Only a couple of times.
jokes.setup: Marge listing Homer's disgusting habits
jokes.punchline: Homer's weak defense of frequency
jokes.explanation: Good gross-out humor with Homer's pathetic attempt to minimize his disgusting behavior

jokes.text: He kicks me in his sleep and his toenails are long and yellow. That's all I can think of.
jokes.setup: The long list of Homer's faults
jokes.punchline: The disgusting final details and casual 'that's all'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with increasingly gross details, ending with Marge's understated conclusion

jokes.text: Out at 5. Catch Sherman at 5:30. Clean him at 6. Eat him by 6:30. Back in bed by 7 with no incriminating evidence. The perfect crime.
jokes.setup: Homer planning to sneak out fishing
jokes.punchline: Calling it 'the perfect crime' for going fishing
jokes.explanation: Homer's overconfident planning and treating fishing like a heist - good character consistency

jokes.text: Go ahead, General Sherman! Waste your strength, you big, ugly catfish. I got a skillet and some butter with your name on it.
jokes.setup: Homer battling the legendary fish
jokes.punchline: His trash talk about cooking the fish
jokes.explanation: Homer's childish trash talk to a fish, showing his competitive nature even in ridiculous situations

jokes.text: For the last time, yes!
jokes.setup: Adult asking if Bart can drink coffee
jokes.punchline: Bart's exasperated confirmation
jokes.explanation: Bart's mature exasperation at being questioned. Mild character moment.

jokes.text: Big blowout at casa de Simpson. The only adult is frail. Bart's joint. Two-ish. Be there or be square.
jokes.setup: Bart organizing a party
jokes.punchline: His use of outdated slang and clinical description of Grampa
jokes.explanation: Good character work with Bart's mix of kid slang and calculating assessment of Grampa's weakness

jokes.text: Tell my friends? All right. But I've got some funky friends.
jokes.setup: Being invited to spread word about party
jokes.punchline: Warning about the quality of his friends
jokes.explanation: Good Nelson characterization - self-aware about his social circle.

jokes.text: As a trained marriage counselor... this is the first time I've told a partner they were right. It's all his fault.
jokes.setup: Professional counselor assessment
jokes.punchline: Breaking professional neutrality to blame Homer entirely
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of professional counseling neutrality - Homer is so bad even the expert takes sides

jokes.text: -Any chicks over 8? -Not yet, but the afternoon is young.
jokes.setup: Nelson asking about girls at the party
jokes.punchline: Bart's optimistic response about attracting older women
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about kids trying to act older, with Bart's entrepreneurial optimism about his party

jokes.text: Thanks for coming. Nice tie, Nelson. It's your dad's.
jokes.setup: Bart complimenting Nelson's formal wear
jokes.punchline: Reveals Nelson stole/borrowed from his father
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Nelson's family situation and his attempt to dress up

jokes.text: It's your dad's.
jokes.setup: Compliment about Nelson's tie
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of wearing his father's clothes
jokes.explanation: Implies Nelson's father is absent/dead. Dark undertone delivered casually.

jokes.text: It's remorse, you burlesque of irrepressible youth. -How do you make it go away? -Grab a brush... and clean faster than you ever have before.
jokes.setup: Bart experiencing guilt for the first time
jokes.punchline: The practical solution of frantic cleaning
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment with Grampa's flowery language and practical wisdom about guilt

jokes.text: -To who? -Those weirdos in the store!
jokes.setup: Homer saying the fish makes him a hero
jokes.punchline: His low standards for heroism and who he wants to impress
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - his desperate need for validation from complete strangers he considers weird

jokes.text: I fought it for six hours. One word and I toss it back. You're telling me our marriage is in trouble?
jokes.setup: Homer's sacrifice of throwing the fish back
jokes.punchline: His incredulity that this doesn't solve everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - one grand gesture should outweigh all other marital problems

jokes.text: Pretending to cry. That's right! You heard me! Pretending to cry! I can turn it on and off like a faucet.
jokes.setup: Asked about his cleaning secret
jokes.punchline: Grampa's proud admission of manipulative fake crying
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character revelation - Grampa's manipulative skills and pride in his deception

jokes.text: "I'm crying. I'm so sad."
jokes.setup: Grampa demonstrating his fake crying
jokes.punchline: The obvious artificiality of his performance
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Grampa's theatrical demonstration of his manipulation technique

jokes.text: His name was Homer. Seven feet tall. Arms like tree trunks. Eyes like steel, cold and hard. Had a shock of hair, red. Like the fires of hell.
jokes.setup: Describing the legendary fisherman who almost caught Sherman
jokes.punchline: The mythic description clearly doesn't match our Homer
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with Homer transformed into a mythic figure completely unlike his actual self
